articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: Goal! VfL Wolfsburg 0, FC Bayern München 1. Jamal Musiala (FC Bayern München) right footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Sacha Boey.
Harry Kane (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card.
Sacha Boey (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! VfL Wolfsburg 1, FC Bayern München 1. Lovro Majer (VfL Wolfsburg) converts the penalty with a left footed shot to the bottom left corner.
Goal! VfL Wolfsburg 2, FC Bayern München 1. Lovro Majer (VfL Wolfsburg) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Patrick Wimmer.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Thomas Müller replaces Sacha Boey.
Own Goal by Jakub Kaminski, VfL Wolfsburg. VfL Wolfsburg 2, FC Bayern München 2.
Jamal Musiala (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.

Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Yannick Gerhardt replaces Lovro Majer because of an injury.

Goal! VfL Wolfsburg 2, FC Bayern München 3. Serge Gnabry (FC Bayern München) right footed shot from the right side of the six yard box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Harry Kane.
Cédric Zesiger (VfL Wolfsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Jonas Wind replaces Mattias Svanberg.

article.headline: Late goal helps Vincent Kompany to first Bayern Munich win
article.description: A late goal by Serge Gnabry secured Bayern Munich a hard-fought 3-2 Bundesliga victory at VfL Wolfsburg on Sunday and gave their new coach Vincent Kompany a winning start.
article.linkText: Late strike helps Kompany to first Bayern win

video.title: Harry Kane picks out Serge Gnabry to fire Bayern in front

article.story:
A late goal by Serge Gnabry secured Bayern Munich a hard-fought 3-2 Bundesliga victory at VfL Wolfsburg on Sunday and gave their new coach Vincent Kompany a winning start.

Wolfsburg began brightly but it was the visitors who made the better chances in the first half, taking the lead after fine work by Frenchman Sacha Boey down the right to set up Jamal Musiala for an easy tap-in.

Bayern made a poor start to the second half, however, as they gave away a penalty when Tiago Tomas was brought down by Boey, and Lovro Majer made no mistake from the spot.

Wolfsburg created all kinds of trouble for the visitors' defence and, in the 55th minute, Patrick Wimmersquared the ball for Majer, who slotted it home to score his second.

Winger Jakub Kaminski's own goal brought Bayern on level terms before Gnabry, assisted by Harry Kane, struck into the corner in the 82nd minute to secure all three points for the Bavarians.

With Kane and new signing Michael Olise looking lively and frequently finding promising positions, Bayern created a host of other chances, but Wolfsburg goalkeeper Kamil Grabara made some brilliant saves to deny the duo.

Thomas Mueller, who equalled a club record with his 709th competitive appearance for Bayern, breathed life back into his side after coming on as a substitute in the 65th minute and was decisive in their win.

The record German champions, who were upstaged last season by Bayer Leverkusen, appointed former Burnley manager Kompany to succeed Thomas Tuchel after their disappointing third-place finish last season.
